name: head_mod_zero_when_aligned
code:
proof fn phi_1_head_mod_zero_when_aligned(info: LogInfo, state: AbstractLogState)
    requires
        info.log_area_len >= MIN_LOG_AREA_SIZE,
        info.head_log_area_offset == info.head as int % info.log_area_len as int,
        info.head as int % info.log_area_len as int == 0,
    ensures
        info.head_log_area_offset == 0,
{
}
reason: When head is perfectly aligned to log_area_len, head_log_area_offset should be 0 — tests that the modular arithmetic base case is consistent

name: no_advancement_preserves_offset
code:
proof fn phi_2_no_advancement_preserves_offset(info: LogInfo, state: AbstractLogState)
    requires
        info.log_area_len >= MIN_LOG_AREA_SIZE,
        info.log_length <= info.log_plus_pending_length <= info.log_area_len,
        info.head_log_area_offset == info.head as int % info.log_area_len as int,
    ensures
        info.head as int % info.log_area_len as int == info.head_log_area_offset,
{
}
reason: Zero advancement (new_head == head) should yield the same offset — tests the trivial identity case of the modular arithmetic

name: advancement_equals_log_area_len_wraps_to_same
reason: Advancing by exactly log_area_len should wrap back to the same offset — if the modular arithmetic doesn't handle full-cycle wrapping, the fast computation would diverge from the true modulus

name: fast_path_matches_slow_path_at_boundary
reason: When advancement exactly reaches the end of the log area, the fast path must take the wrapping branch — if the branch condition is off-by-one, the boundary case would compute an out-of-range offset

name: lemma_small_advancement_no_wrap
code:
proof fn phi_5_lemma_small_advancement_no_wrap(info: LogInfo, state: AbstractLogState, advance: u64)
    requires
        info.log_area_len >= MIN_LOG_AREA_SIZE,
        info.log_length <= info.log_plus_pending_length <= info.log_area_len,
        info.head_log_area_offset == info.head as int % info.log_area_len as int,
        0 < advance <= info.log_length,
        advance < info.log_area_len - info.head_log_area_offset,
        info.head + advance as u128 <= u128::MAX,
    ensures
        (info.head + advance as u128) as int % info.log_area_len as int == (info.head_log_area_offset + advance) as int,
{
    UntrustedLogImpl::lemma_check_fast_way_to_compute_head_mod_log_area_len(info, state, (info.head + advance as u128) as u128);
}
reason: For small advancements (no wrapping), the new offset should simply be the old offset plus the advancement — tests that the non-wrapping branch of the fast computation is a simple addition
